text: Last class you reported what other people said.
Today you give your opinion and connect every idea clearly.
However
Moreover
In addition
Furthermore
Therefore
On the other hand
Nevertheless
For instance
As a result
In conclusion
Despite
Whereas
It is cheap; moreover, it is fast.
I like art; however, I dislike exams.
He trained daily; therefore, he won.
Some sports help, for instance, swimming.
Mark, we present our opinion tomorrow. How should we start our argument?
First we state our view clearly. Moreover, we add reasons. For instance, students learn more with breaks.
Good. In addition, we should mention the other side, so the teacher sees we are fair.
Exactly. We can say: however, some people disagree, and on the other hand, longer breaks cost time.
Nice. Therefore, we sound balanced, whereas last time we only gave one side.
Right. In conclusion, we repeat our main point. As a result, our argument will be strong and clear.
In my view, schools should start later.
Moreover, students would feel less tired.
However, some parents disagree.
For instance, sports could finish after dark.
In conclusion, the benefits are stronger.

Good morning, students. This is your school radio editorial. Today I want to give you my honest opinion about a popular idea: should our school start one hour later? In my view, the answer is yes. First of all, many teenagers go to bed late and wake up tired. Therefore, a later start could help us focus better in the morning. Moreover, several studies show that students who sleep more get higher grades. For instance, one school in another city tried a later start and saw much better results. However, I understand the other side. Some parents work early and cannot drive their children later. In addition, sports practice might finish after dark. Nevertheless, I believe the benefits are stronger than the problems. In conclusion, a later start would make us healthier and more focused. Thank you for listening, and have a great day.

Hi everyone, and welcome back to the Teen Voices podcast. In today's short episode, I want to share my opinion on a simple question: are team sports better than individual sports for teenagers? Personally, I think both are valuable, but team sports have a special advantage. To begin with, they teach you to work with other people and to trust your teammates. In addition, they make exercise feel fun rather than boring. For instance, a tough training session feels much easier when your friends are next to you. On the other hand, individual sports like running or swimming give you more freedom and a flexible schedule. Some people love that independence, whereas others feel lonely without a team. Despite these differences, the most important thing is simply to stay active. As a result, my advice is easy: choose the sport you enjoy, and keep moving. That is all for today. See you next week.

First, you state your opinion clearly. Then you give your first reason. Next, you add a second reason with moreover or in addition. After that, you mention the other side with however or on the other hand. Finally, you sum up your view with in conclusion.

First of all
I enjoy science; however, I find history boring.
The idea is cheap. Moreover, it is fast.

Reading is fun. In addition, it builds your vocabulary.
Despite the rain, we played outside.
I like tennis, whereas my brother likes football.
In my view, schools should start later.
Moreover, students would feel less tired.
However, some parents disagree.
In conclusion, the benefits are stronger.
For instance, sports could finish after dark.
